text:
Sure. In a realm where time flowed like river waters, there lived an old wolf named Wisper. Wisper had deep blue eyes that twinkled like starlight. He'd listen patiently to the tales of ravens.

One autumn evening, the sky filled with gold. A gentle breeze carried the sound of laughter. Wisper listened.

Children's voices sang about a magical river. It glimmered in the moonlight. It brought dreams true.

Among them was a clever little fox thief named Tick. Tick liked sneaking through enchanted gardens.

One night, Tick decided to dive into the river.

"Oh, how lovely!" sighed Wisper, sensing Tick.

But Tick fell. Into a enchanted swamp.

"Wake up!"

It was his own voice.

He popped out shiny-eyed.

"Or maybe you're dreaming?"

Wesper chuckled.

Tick decided dreams were treasure.

He swam back. Wisper applauded.

"You dream big! Like dreams?"

With a waggle.

Together they dreamed of
